name: How long does a full siding replacement usually take from start to finish?
headline: How long does a full siding replacement usually take from start to finish?

mainEntity.name: How long does a full siding replacement usually take from start to finish?
mainEntity.text: How long does a full siding replacement usually take from start to finish?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a typical single‑family home, a siding replacement usually takes **1 to 3 weeks of on‑site work**, depending on size, complexity, and weather. Here’s a rough timeline: - 1–3 days: Set up, protect landscaping, and remove old siding. - Several days: Inspect and repair any damaged sheathing or framing, install housewrap and flashings. - 3–10 days: Install new siding, trim, vents, and accessories. - 1–3 days: Caulking, painting or staining (if needed), and final touch‑ups. On taller or more complex homes in Hillsborough or San Bruno, scaffolding and multiple stories add time. On the coast, fog and rain can slow down painting or caulking days. Permit inspections can also add waiting time if schedules don’t line up. Common pitfalls: - No clear schedule or communication, leading to open walls longer than necessary. - Starting without materials on hand, then pausing for back‑orders. - Overlapping trades (like painters and siding installers) in ways that create confusion or mistakes. Before starting, ask for a realistic timeline, how many workers will be on site, how they handle weather delays, and what parts of the project might extend the schedule. Build in a small buffer so you’re not surprised if it runs a bit longer.
